project.name: date-selector-from-dataset-date-availability
project.description: A pro-code Domo custom app that renders a calendar grid (or compact dropdown) of dates available in a bound dataset, writing the user's selection to a Domo App Studio page variable so other cards filter accordingly. Built with React 18, TypeScript, Vite, and ryuu.js v6, with AppDB persisting the variable functionId per app instance.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry point that mounts the React root and loads src/main.tsx as the application bootstrap module.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM package manifest declaring React 18, ryuu.js, Vite, and date-fns dependencies along with dev/build/preview scripts for the Domo custom app.

nodes.name: manifest.json
nodes.summary: Domo custom app manifest declaring dataset mappings, AppDB collection wiring, and runtime sizing so the app can bind to a date dataset and persist per-instance settings.
nodes.languageNotes: Domo manifest with datasetsMapping for the bound date dataset and collectionsMapping for AppDB-backed settings persistence.

nodes.name: sample-data.csv
nodes.summary: Local mock dataset of sample dates used during IS_LOCAL development to simulate the bound Domo dataset before publishing.

nodes.name: sample-variables-registry.csv
nodes.summary: Local registry CSV mapping App Studio variable names to numeric function IDs, used in IS_LOCAL mode to test variable-name-based selection.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Developer guide covering stack, local dev workflow, Domo publish steps, per-page configuration (functionId discovery), manifest fields, and troubleshooting for the date-selector custom app.

nodes.name: App.css
nodes.summary: Component-level stylesheet defining the calendar grid, dropdown, settings panel, and responsive layout for the date selector UI.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Core React component implementing the date-selector custom app — fetches available dates from the bound dataset, renders calendar/dropdown UI, manages single/range selection, writes back to App Studio variables via ryuu, and persists settings via AppDB.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-file React 18 component (~1100 lines) combining ryuu.js variable I/O, AppDB CRUD with localStorage fallback, and date-fns-driven calendar rendering.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: React 18 bootstrap that mounts the App component into the DOM root and imports the global stylesheet.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the React/Vite source tree with strict typing and modern ES module resolution.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration variant scoped to Node-side tooling files such as vite.config.ts.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration enabling the React plugin for the custom app's dev server and production bundle.

nodes.name: toISO
nodes.summary: Converts a JavaScript Date into a zero-padded YYYY-MM-DD ISO string for comparison against dataset date strings.

nodes.name: isoToDate
nodes.summary: Parses a YYYY-MM-DD string into a local-time Date, avoiding the timezone shift that new Date(iso) would introduce.

nodes.name: fetchLocalRegistry
nodes.summary: Fetches and parses the local sample-variables-registry CSV into a Map of variable name to function ID for IS_LOCAL development.

nodes.name: resolveVarIds
nodes.summary: Resolves variable name to functionId mappings either from the local CSV (IS_LOCAL) or from the published variables-registry dataset via the Domo data API, caching the result.

nodes.name: fetchLocalDates
nodes.summary: Fetches and parses the local sample-data CSV to extract the sorted list of unique ISO dates for IS_LOCAL mode.

nodes.name: registerVariablesListener
nodes.summary: Subscribes to ryuu's onVariablesUpdated event to discover live App Studio variables on the host page and ingest their current values for the settings picker.

nodes.name: discoverViaPageControls
nodes.summary: Discovers variables defined on the current App Studio page by querying the page controls endpoint and extracting functionId plus name pairs.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root React component orchestrating settings load, date fetching, variable discovery, calendar/dropdown UI rendering, range selection logic, and ryuu variable writeback for the date selector.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: React entry point, main App component, and global styles rendering the calendar/list view and settings panel inside the Domo brick.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Build, TypeScript, npm, and Domo manifest configuration that wire the Vite bundle, AppDB collection, and dataset bindings for the custom app.

layers.name: Local Mock Data
layers.description: CSV fixtures used only in IS_LOCAL mode to emulate the Domo-bound sample dataset and variables registry during Vite dev.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Developer-facing README covering setup, IS_LOCAL flow, publish via ryuu, and per-page variable-ID discovery.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: main.tsx is the React entry point — it mounts the <App /> component into the #root element with StrictMode. This is where the browser-rendered tree begins inside the Domo iframe, kicked off by index.html loading the module. Everything else in the tour hangs off this single bootstrap file.
tour.languageLesson: React 18's createRoot API replaces the legacy ReactDOM.render. StrictMode intentionally double-invokes effects in development to surface side-effect bugs — expected, harmless in production.

tour.title: Project Purpose & Local Dev Flow
tour.description: The README orients you to what this app is: a Domo App Studio custom card that renders only the dates present in a bound dataset and writes the user's pick to a page variable. It also documents the IS_LOCAL flag pattern, the publish flow via the Domo CLI, and the browser-console snippet used to discover the page variable's functionId. Read this before diving into App.tsx so the variable-discovery code makes sense.

tour.title: The Heart of the App — App.tsx
tour.description: App.tsx is where everything happens: it fetches the bound dataset via ryuu.js, derives the distinct available dates, renders either a react-day-picker calendar or a compact list view, and pushes the user's selection back through domo.requestVariablesUpdate. It also hosts the settings panel that persists the variable functionId to AppDB and auto-detects variable updates via domo.onVariablesUpdated. Every other file in the tour exists to support this component.
tour.languageLesson: Domo's ryuu.js v6 exposes domo.get, domo.post, domo.onVariablesUpdated, and domo.requestVariablesUpdate. These calls are no-ops outside the Domo iframe — that's why an IS_LOCAL escape hatch is needed for Vite dev.

tour.title: Styling the Calendar
tour.description: App.css holds the global styles plus the theme overrides for react-day-picker (the NPM dependency that renders the actual calendar grid). It is imported by both main.tsx and App.tsx so the day-picker visual customizations apply wherever the calendar mounts. Reading this clarifies which DOM hooks the calendar exposes for theming.

tour.title: Domo Platform Contract — manifest.json
tour.description: manifest.json is the single most important config file for a Domo custom app: it declares the design id (which identifies the published app on the Domo instance), the datasetsMapping that wires the SampleData and variablesDataSet aliases used inside App.tsx, and the collectionsMapping that provisions the AppDB collection the settings panel reads and writes. Without these mappings the Domo runtime would not pass any data or grant any AppDB access to the iframe.
tour.languageLesson: datasetsMapping aliases let App.tsx call domo.get('/data/v1/sampleData') without hard-coding a dataset GUID — the customer rebinds the alias to their real dataset at install time. collectionsMapping similarly abstracts the AppDB collection name from the app code.

tour.title: Dependencies & Scripts — package.json
tour.description: package.json declares the runtime dependencies (React 18, ryuu.js v6 for the Domo SDK, date-fns for date math, react-day-picker for the calendar UI) and the dev toolchain (Vite + TypeScript). The scripts here drive `npm run dev` (Vite local preview) and `npm run build` (production bundle that gets `domo publish`-ed from dist/).

tour.title: Build Configuration — Vite & TypeScript
tour.description: vite.config.ts sets up the React plugin and base path so the build output is loadable from inside the Domo iframe. tsconfig.json governs how App.tsx and main.tsx are type-checked, while tsconfig.node.json applies to the tooling side (vite.config.ts itself). Together they form the build pipeline that turns the source into a publishable bundle.

tour.title: Local Mock Data
tour.description: When Vite runs locally there is no Domo iframe to serve datasets, so App.tsx uses an IS_LOCAL branch that fetches sample-data.csv (the dates fixture) and sample-variables-registry.csv (the optional variable-name registry) from /public. The Domo runtime ignores these files entirely — they only exist to make the dev loop work without a live Domo connection. This is the dev-time analogue of the manifest.json datasetsMapping covered in Step 5.
tour.languageLesson: CSV mocks shipped under public/ are a common pattern for client-only apps with no backend — Vite serves them as static assets, and an IS_LOCAL env check toggles between fetch('/sample-data.csv') and the platform SDK call.
